must_detect.matched_text:
On 2024-05-28, our internal monitoring in DIFC detected anomalous access to the Finance drive by Ms. Leila Barakat (Deputy Finance Manager, age 34, DOB 1989-11-17) via a company laptop.
Device: Dell Latitude 7420 asset tag SH-DW-7420-33; VPN sess id sh-vpn/ab3c-9 4; IPv4 10.18.7.44; not a MAC address: 00-00-00-00-00-00.
Ms. Barakat’s corporate account leila.barakat@saharhorizons.example accessed https://intra.saharhorizons.example/fin/term-sheet?deal=SL-sukuk&sid=se s s-4F12C9 and uploaded a zip to share-ext.
Access originated from a hotel Wi-Fi in Al Maryah Island, Abu Dhabi (ADGM vicinity), during a roadshow prep meeting.
Her profile notes family roots in Al Ain (maternal), Liwa Oasis (paternal), which are irrelevant to this assessment but stored in HR for diversity reporting.
HR fields include Emirates ID ref EID 784-1989-1234567-3 and Trade Licence TL-AD-923451 for Sahar Horizons PJSC; no original IDs were exported.
The files touched included an unsigned ADX timetable and draft sustainability-linked sukuk allocation model for Al Qamar Renewables LLC (issuer mandate pending), which is confidential MNPI under SCA/ADGM guidance.
Public data such as 2023 ESG policy brochures and generic procurement templates are not sensitive and can remain on the shared drive.
We did not observe credential stuffing, and no payment card numbers or health data were processed; the string 1234-56-789 is a product code, not an account.
A separate spreadsheet lists “DOB 1975-02-05; role: analyst; device: iPhone 12; location: Sharjah” without names and should not be treated as directly identifying.
Export log row: ac c t: 400-77 21-ABD, bucket=fin-esg-v5, u r l: https://dl.saharhorizons.example/esg/v5/score?proj=ne w-w ind&rid=R-0 08.
Generic demographics in the risk dashboard (male 40s, marina commute, Android user) are non-actionable alone and do not create identifiability per PDPL.
As per DIFC Data Protection Law and UAE PDPL, we will notify only if confirmed exfiltration of PD falls above risk threshold; at present, indicators show limited exposure linked to a single user and device.
For follow-up, contact Dr. Omar Safwan, DPO, at dpo@saharhorizons.example or +971-50-000-1234; this report is marked Internal—Market Abuse Controls.
This incident does not indicate market manipulation, does not include pricing guidance, and does not reveal a securities identifier or ISIN.
